sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, data BITSTRING, created_at TIME, value DECIMAL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at, value) VALUES (1, '1101011'::BITSTRING, TIME '09:00:00', 123.456);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at, value) VALUES (2, '101010'::BITSTRING, TIME '11:30:00', 789.012);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at, value) VALUES (3, '1111111'::BITSTRING, TIME '15:00:00', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, data, created_at, value) VALUES (4, '0000000'::BITSTRING, NULL, 0.0);
SELECT id, bit_length(data) + INTERVAL (DECIMAL(5.0) * value) HOUR AS adjusted_time FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, data, bit_length(data) AS bit_len FROM test_table WHERE bit_length(data) + 1 > 7;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY bit_length(data)) AS bit_count FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, bit_length(data) + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS new_time FROM test_table ORDER BY new_time DESC;
WITH adjusted_times AS (SELECT id, created_at + INTERVAL bit_length(data) HOUR AS adjusted_time FROM test_table) SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(HOUR FROM adjusted_time)) AS avg_hour FROM adjusted_times;
SELECT DISTINCT A.id FROM test_table A JOIN test_table B ON bit_length(A.data) = bit_length(B.data) WHERE A.id != B.id;
SELECT id, bit_length(data) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NULL HAVING bit_length(data) < 6;
SELECT id, bit_length(data) AS length FROM test_table WHERE created_at BETWEEN TIME '00:00:00' AND TIME '23:59:59' ORDER BY length;
